# Contributing (Issue Hygiene)

Use GitHub Issue Forms for all new work so planning stays consistent across repositories.

## Which template to use
- **Bug**: broken behavior, regressions, production defects
- **Task**: implementation/refactor/cleanup/operations work
- **Feature**: net-new product capability or enhancement

## Required planning fields
Every issue should include:
- Problem / Why
- Scope and Out of Scope
- Priority (`P0`-`P3`)
- Size (`XS/S/M/L/XL`)
- Estimate (days)
- Acceptance Criteria (checkboxes)
- Dependencies / Blockers
- Verification Plan

## Sizing standard
- `XS`: < 1 day
- `S`: 1-2 days
- `M`: 3-4 days
- `L`: 5-6 days
- `XL`: 7+ days

## Status flow (project board)
`Freezer` -> `Backlog` -> `Ready` -> `In Progress` -> `In Review` -> `Complete`

## Linking related work
- Add a **Related Issues** section with direct links to dependent/coupled tasks.
- Add a **Subtasks** checklist for child tasks when work can be split.
- Keep relationships current as issues move across statuses.
